title: LINFO2145 Cloud Computing Project
description: Collaborative (pair) project: development of a cloud application based on a microservices architecture with Docker. Implemented an automated CI/CD deployment pipeline on Azure and advanced administration features.
longDescription: Cloud-native application developed as part of the LINFO2145 - Cloud Computing course. The project implements a microservices architecture (API gateway, cart service, command journaling for asynchronous orders, products for sale, authentication service, etc.) with automated deployment on Azure and a MapReduce system to optimize the recommendation service.
challenges: The main challenges of this project were implementing a scalable microservices architecture while starting from an already advanced project provided by the instructor. We first had to run the project locally, then add our own services and features. Extensive testing was also carried out to ensure the local version behaves the same way as the instructor's.

title: Thesis - Carbon Calculator
description: Master's thesis on developing an intelligent carbon footprint calculator with AI and environmental data analysis.
longDescription: Master's thesis focused on developing an intelligent carbon footprint calculator. The project combines machine learning, data analysis, and modern user interface to raise awareness about environmental issues and provide concrete solutions for emission reduction.
challenges: Development of an innovative predictive model combining behavioral data and machine learning algorithms to optimize carbon footprint reduction recommendations.

title: Node.js Userland-Migrations - LINFO2401
description: Contribution to Node.js project to automate migration from deprecated TLS API (SecurePair) to the new API (TLSSocket).
longDescription: Major contribution to the official Node.js userland-migrations project as part of LINFO2401 course. Developed an automated transformation script (codemod) to migrate code using tls.SecurePair (deprecated) to tls.TLSSocket. This project helps thousands of developers update their code during major Node.js API changes.
challenges: In-depth AST (Abstract Syntax Tree) analysis, mapping between cleartext/encrypted streams to unified TLSSocket API, managing multi-platform compatibility (Windows CI tests), and collaborating with Node.js maintainers to align with internal standards.

title: Image Segmentation - Cats & Dogs
description: Image segmentation project using U-Net and MobileNetV2 to create binary masks of cat and dog silhouettes. Optimization with transfer learning and test-time augmentation.
longDescription: Improvement of an image segmentation model to detect and extract silhouettes of cats and dogs. The project uses a U-Net architecture with MobileNetV2 as a pre-trained encoder on ImageNet, skip connections, and various optimization techniques including grid search, test-time augmentation and uncertainty analysis.
challenges: Avoid overfitting, optimize encoder-decoder architecture with skip connections, handle low contrast between animal and background, and improve accuracy with limited data via transfer learning.

title: CGAN - Conditional MNIST Image Generation
description: Conditional GAN implementation to generate class-controlled MNIST digits. Adversarial architecture with conditional embedding, BCE loss, and FID/IS metrics.
longDescription: Research project on CGANs applied to MNIST dataset. Implementation of label-conditioned generator-discriminator architecture with embedding, Adam optimization, and evaluation via Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) and Inception Score (IS). Exploration of mode collapse, hyperparameters, and stabilization strategies.
challenges: Balancing generator/discriminator training, preventing mode collapse, optimizing learning rates (0.0002), latent space dimensioning (100D), and qualitative/quantitative evaluation.

title: Skijo - Skyjo implementation
description: Python implementation of the Skyjo card game with Tkinter UI, scoring system and multiplayer logic.
longDescription: Skijo is a digital implementation of Skyjo. The project includes game logic, a Tkinter UI, round and score management, and a bot skeleton.
challenges: Game state management, initial reveal phase and bot AI improvements.

title: Hackathon Odoo 2025
description: Visual cryptography web application to create a time capsule hidden inside memes, unlockable only by the intended audience. Project developed in a team of 3.
longDescription: KAPsule Temporelle was born from a hackathon challenge: design a digital time capsule that can be published on the Internet but only decrypted by targeted recipients. To ensure longevity, we leveraged the idea that the Internet retains content and hid messages inside persistent online content (e.g. popular memes such as certain Shrek memes). The operational goal was that only students from Louvain-la-Neuve could unlock the capsule. To achieve this we combined steganography and Fernet encryption with behavioral authentication based on expression and motion detection (e.g. recognizing a happy person performing a specific gesture). My contribution focused on implementing and integrating the image recognition system (YOLO v8, MediaPipe, DeepFace) and coupling it with a FastAPI backend.

title: Game Jam 2023
description: Narrative platformer game prototype developed in 24h with Godot during Game Jam 2023. 2nd place achieved. Team project with 4 members (2 developers, 2 graphic designers).
longDescription: Project completed during Game Jam 2023 (April 22-23, 2023), a contest organized by the student project house 'Le Linux', with the theme of non-violent solutions. Narrative platformer prototype centered on Bobbi, an unstable little cat who must climb back from the bottom of a cave while avoiding hostile inhabitants. Eliminating enemies is easy, but the real objective is to exit the well using as little violence as possible to avoid descending into madness. Developed with Godot in a team of 4: Maxime Devillet and Alix Temmerman (developers), Céline Demaret and Silvia Spasova (graphic designers). Black & white art direction with pink accents, 2.5D effect and unsettling atmosphere inspired by Badland and Limbo.
challenges: Creating a unique atmosphere in 24h, managing platformer mechanics, designing memorable characters, and creating multiple levels with narrative progression.

title: English Teacher 3 - Automated Anki Generator
description: Automated English vocabulary learning system that generates Anki decks from movie and TV show subtitles. Uses AI and NLP to extract and filter B2-C1 level vocabulary.
longDescription: English Teacher 3 is an automation project for English vocabulary learning. The system analyzes movie and TV show subtitles, extracts relevant vocabulary using SpaCy, filters by CEFR level (B2-C1), and automatically generates enriched Anki cards with translations, definitions, examples, and audio pronunciation.
challenges: Optimizing linguistic filtering to avoid 88% noise, integrating multiple APIs (Dictionary, TTS), intelligent audio cache management and automated CEFR scoring system.
